successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest exactly as defined in the architecture specification.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository with create, findById, findByEmployeeId, and update methods.
src/modules/leave/postgresql-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgreSqlLeaveRequestRepository implementing LeaveRequestRepository.

A PostgreSQL schema definition or migration creates the leave_requests table with columns id, employee_id, leave_type, start_date, end_date, status, created_at, and updated_at matching the provided schema.
All LeaveRequest persistence operations are accessed through the LeaveRequestRepository abstraction, with PostgreSQL-specific access confined to the repository implementation.
Vitest tests cover repository create, findById, findByEmployeeId, and update behaviors.
Project architecture documentation is updated to include the LeaveRequest entity, LeaveType values ANNUAL/SICK/EMERGENCY, and LeaveRequestStatus values PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED.

constraints: Limit implementation to LeaveRequest persistence and type definitions within the leave module.
Do not implement submitLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest, or any workflow logic.
Repository pattern: all database access for LeaveRequest must go through the LeaveRequestRepository interface.
PostgreSQL-specific persistence details must remain inside the repository implementation.
Audit records are required for state-changing operations by project standards; however, no audit module implementation should be introduced in this phase.
No sensitive data may be logged.
No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors must be caught and handled.
Maintain module architecture boundaries; do not introduce reverse dependencies from employee, policy, balance, or notification modules into leave.
Use TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Vitest consistent with the project architecture.
Do not expose direct PostgreSQL access contracts outside the repository implementation.
outOfScope: AuditRecord model, repository, or audit module implementation.
Leave balance deductions or balance module changes.
Any functionality scheduled for phases 2 through 8 beyond documentation and persistence scaffolding defined in this phase.

ambiguities.description: The intent specifies approximately 4-5 files under src/modules/leave but also requires a leave_requests PostgreSQL table and architecture documentation update, without specifying exact file locations for schema migration and documentation changes.
options: Create additional migration and documentation files as needed outside src/modules/leave.
Represent schema/documentation updates using existing project mechanisms if available, keeping leave module files to approximately 4-5.

ambiguities.description: Repository tests are requested, but the execution style is not specified.
options: Use integration-style tests against a PostgreSQL test database.
Use mocked or in-memory persistence-focused tests validating repository behavior.
